nodes.label: AI integration into workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I use AI in a wide variety of ways in a typical day

nodes.label: AI as conversational journal and task manager
nodes.label_negative: AI as impersonal tool without emotional support
nodes.grounding_span: I use it sort of like a journal, but one that can teach me things

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes a model will just not listen to a rule that I state over and over again

nodes.label: AI as mentor for learning
nodes.grounding_span: I feel like I work more on learning new skills, usually AI skills

nodes.label: AI as assistant for complex work
nodes.grounding_span: AI will do more of the tasks I currently do, and then I'll check on it once a day

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I find this helps me stay on task

nodes.label: Personal well-being and reduced cognitive load
nodes.grounding_span: I use it to track work things and personal things, so it can remind me to take my vitamins

nodes.label: Continuous learning and adaptation
nodes.grounding_span: I work more on learning new skills, usually AI skills

nodes.label: Autonomy and personal control
nodes.grounding_span: my life will be less like working from say 9 am to 5 pm

nodes.label: Desire for more capable AI tools
nodes.grounding_span: There is really no tasks I would prefer to do myself

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's failure to follow rules
nodes.grounding_span: Sometimes a model will just not listen to a rule that I state over and over again, and this makes me crazy

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I think that over time, AI will do more of the tasks I currently do

nodes.label: Opportunistic task-switching across platforms
nodes.grounding_span: I usually have about 10 tabs open and Im switching between them all

nodes.label: Proactive skill positioning — strategically uses AI to maintain relevance
nodes.grounding_span: I work more on learning new skills, usually AI skills, and less on maintaining existing skills
